tasks.subject: [agent-budget-enforcement] [P1] Delete AgentBudgetSchema + barrel re-export + every compiler-guided production site (registry, reconciler, 8 seed sites) — the keystone sweep
tasks.description: Delete AgentBudgetSchema and every production-code site that references it — the compiler-guided keystone sweep (schema first, then every typed site the deletion turns red). SCHEMA (packages/shared/src/mesh-schemas.ts): delete :69-84, the AgentBudgetSchema TSDoc block carrying the "not currently enforced at runtime… treat them as advisory metadata" NOTE; delete :85-92, `export const AgentBudgetSchema = z.object({ maxHopsPerMessage: …, maxCallsPerHour: … })` and `export type AgentBudget = z.infer<typeof AgentBudgetSchema>`; delete the line at :163, `budget: AgentBudgetSchema.default(...)`, inside `AgentManifestSchema`; delete the line at :342, `budget: true`, inside `UpdateAgentRequestSchema.pick({...})`. Delete the `AgentBudget` re-export at packages/shared/src/types.ts:170 from the shared barrel. Leave `AgentBehaviorSchema` (mesh-schemas.ts:60-67) completely untouched — it is a live, unrelated field and is the model the surviving schema follows. Do NOT touch packages/db/src/schema/mesh.ts in this task (the `budgetJson` column definition and its migration are task 2.1's sole scope) — only remove the CODE that reads/writes it below. REGISTRY (packages/mesh/src/agent-registry.ts): delete :110, `budgetJson: JSON.stringify(agent.budget)`, from the insert values; delete :132, the `budgetJson: …` entry inside `onConflictDoUpdate`'s set; delete :222, the `budgetJson: …` entry inside `update()`; delete :434, `budget: JSON.parse(row.budgetJson)`, inside `rowToEntry` (AgentRegistryEntry extends AgentManifest, so its `budget` member vanishes automatically once the shared type is gone — this is the return-type compile error the deletion fixes). RECONCILER (packages/mesh/src/reconciler.ts): delete :130, `budget: manifest.budget`, from the sync payload; delete the clause at :206, `JSON.stringify(manifest.budget) !== JSON.stringify(entry.budget) ||`, from the field-diff — keep the surrounding `behavior`/`persona` comparisons untouched. EIGHT SEED SITES that hardcode `budget: { maxHopsPerMessage: 5, maxCallsPerHour: 100 }` — delete the line/block at each: apps/server/src/routes/agents.ts:135; apps/server/src/routes/test-control.ts:81; apps/server/src/services/mesh/ensure-dorkbot.ts:68 (DorkBot system-agent manifest); apps/server/src/services/core/agent-creator.ts:264; packages/mesh/src/mesh-discovery.ts:104 (the `register()` manifest builder) and :148 (the `registerByPath()` manifest builder); apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/direct/mesh-methods.ts:49 (DirectTransport's manifest builder — typed `AgentManifest`, so this is compiler-guided too); packages/test-utils/src/mock-factories.ts:114 (the shared mock-manifest factory — its removal propagates the fixture change to every test that consumes the factory rather than each test inlining its own budget field). Because this task removes the ONLY sites that read or write `AgentRegistryEntry.budget`/`Manifest.budget` inside the mesh package, also fix the three mesh tests that directly assert on this code so the task is self-verifying: packages/mesh/src/__tests__/agent-registry.test.ts:30,186,190,236,262 — remove the round-trip asserts (`expect(entry?.budget.maxCallsPerHour).toBe(10)`, custom-budget-persisted cases) and their budget inputs; packages/mesh/src/__tests__/relay-integration.test.ts:322,329,337 — remove `expect(manifest.budget).toEqual(...)` / `expect(agent!.budget).toEqual(...)`; packages/mesh/src/__tests__/reconciler.test.ts — remove any assertion that the field-diff flags a budget-only difference as changed (keep the behavior/persona diff coverage intact — the diff clause removal must not regress sync detection for the fields that remain). Acceptance: AgentBudgetSchema/AgentBudget no longer exist anywhere in @dorkos/shared; no production file outside packages/db (task 2.1's scope) references `.budget`, `budgetJson`, `maxHopsPerMessage`, or `maxCallsPerHour`; agent-registry.test.ts, relay-integration.test.ts, and reconciler.test.ts pass with no budget assertions. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build && pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/mesh typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/mesh lint && pnpm vitest run packages/mesh/src/__tests__/agent-registry.test.ts packages/mesh/src/__tests__/relay-integration.test.ts packages/mesh/src/__tests__/reconciler.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/test-utils typecheck. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 1 (schema — the keystone), §2 (registry/reconciler code sites; the DB column and its migration are out of scope here, owned by task 2.1), §3 (all eight seed sites), §8 (the agent-registry/relay-integration/reconciler test-assertion removals), §Testing Strategy (Mesh — registry round-trip, Mesh — reconciler, Mesh — relay integration), Implementation Phases 1 and 3. No dependencies — this is the keystone every other task waits on.
tasks.activeForm: Deleting AgentBudgetSchema and sweeping every compiler-guided production site

tasks.phaseName: P2 — Persistence: drop the budget_json column
tasks.subject: [agent-budget-enforcement] [P2] Drop the agents.budget_json column — generated Drizzle migration + rewritten migration test
tasks.description: Drop the `agents.budget_json` column via a generated Drizzle migration and rewrite its migration test to prove the column is gone, not silently dropped. Edit packages/db/src/schema/mesh.ts:23-25 to delete the `budgetJson` column definition from the `agents` table (task 1.1 has already removed every production read/write of this column in packages/mesh/src/agent-registry.ts and reconciler.ts, so this edit strands no live code). Generate the migration — do NOT hand-write SQL: run `pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:generate`, which produces `packages/db/drizzle/00NN_<name>.sql` (next index is `0028`; drizzle assigns the slug) containing `ALTER TABLE \`agents\` DROP COLUMN \`budget_json\`;`, plus a new `packages/db/drizzle/meta/00NN_snapshot.json` and a new `_journal.json` entry. Open the generated `.sql` file and verify its ONLY statement is the column drop — nothing else drifted — before considering the migration correct. Replace the test at packages/db/src/__tests__/migrations.test.ts:147-165 ("agents table has budget_json column with default hop and rate limits") with a new test that runs `runMigrations` on a fresh `:memory:` database and asserts `PRAGMA table_info(agents)` contains NO `budget_json` column, and that an INSERT omitting `budget` succeeds. Acceptance: the generated migration file's only statement is the `budget_json` column drop; `meta/00NN_snapshot.json` and `_journal.json` are updated; the rewritten migrations.test.ts proves the column is absent post-migration and an insert without budget succeeds; @dorkos/db typechecks clean. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:generate (confirm no further diff — it was already run to produce the migration) && pnpm --filter @dorkos/db typecheck && pnpm vitest run packages/db/src/__tests__/migrations.test.ts. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 2 (persistence — the generated migration, exact SQL, and the migration-test rewrite), §Technical Dependencies (drizzle-kit db:generate, SQLite 3.35+ DROP COLUMN support), §Testing Strategy (DB — migration). Depends on 1.1 (the registry/reconciler code that read/wrote `budgetJson` must be gone first, so the column drop strands no live reference). Runs in parallel with 3.1 (client UI), 4.1 (docs/OpenAPI), and 5.1 (changelog/ADR) — a different package, zero file overlap.
tasks.activeForm: Dropping the agents.budget_json column via a generated migration

tasks.subject: [agent-budget-enforcement] [P3] Remove all client UI budget surfaces (ToolsTab Limits, AgentRow, topology ×3) + 3 dev showcases + tests
tasks.description: Remove every client UI surface that displays or edits the agent budget — the editable ToolsTab "Limits" section, the AgentRow line, and the three topology displays — plus the three dev showcases, leaving an honest absence: no placeholder, no orphaned divider, no empty state for a limit that does not exist. TOOLSTAB (apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/ToolsTab.tsx) — remove the whole "Limits" block: :210 the docstring clause "and collapsible safety limits (budget)"; :213 `const [limitsOpen, setLimitsOpen] = useState(false);`; :283-284 the `const hops`/`const calls` reads; :351-401 the `CollapsibleFieldCard` "Limits" (badge `{hops} hops · {calls} calls/hr` plus two `SettingRow`s with `` for `maxHopsPerMessage`/`maxCallsPerHour`, each calling `onUpdate({ budget: … })`). Drop the now-unused imports: `CollapsibleFieldCard` (only use) and `Input` (only use); `useState` becomes unused and drops from the `react` import. KEEP `SettingRow` — it is still used at :132 and :152 by `ToolGroupRow`. The tab ends after MCP Servers with nothing replacing the removed section. AGENTROW (apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/ui/AgentRow.tsx:213-219) — delete the `{/* Budget */}` block ("Budget: max {maxHopsPerMessage} hops · {maxCallsPerHour} calls/hr"), guard and div together; the sibling response-mode and namespace rows are untouched. TOPOLOGY (features/mesh, three coupled files) — apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/lib/build-topology-elements.ts:167-172, delete the `budget: agent.budget ? {…} : undefined` node-data mapping; apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/ui/AgentNode.tsx:28, delete the local `budget?: {…}` field on the node-data type, and :202, delete the `{d.budget.maxCallsPerHour} calls/hr · {d.budget.maxHopsPerMessage} max hops` render; apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/ui/TopologyPanel.tsx:54-55, delete the two `{…} calls/hr` / `max hops` displays. DEV SHOWCASES (loosely typed, so they escape the compiler and must be found by file:line, not by the compiler sweep — see the maintaining-dev-playground skill) — apps/client/src/dev/showcases/topology-agent-node.tsx:20 (local type field), :137 (display), :168 (mock data); apps/client/src/dev/showcases/settings-mock-data.ts:115,152; apps/client/src/dev/showcases/FilterBarShowcase.tsx:23,47,71,95,119,143 (six mock agents) — delete every listed budget reference. TESTS — apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/__tests__/ToolsTab.test.tsx: remove any test exercising the Limits inputs, and keep/add a test asserting the tab renders tool groups + MCP servers with the "Limits" control NOT present; apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/__tests__/AgentRow.test.tsx:132: drop budget from fixtures, assert the budget line is absent and the surrounding row still renders; apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/lib/__tests__/build-topology-elements.test.ts:21: drop budget from fixtures, assert budget data is absent from the node mapping. Acceptance: no "Limits"/"Budget" UI surface remains in ToolsTab, AgentRow, AgentNode, TopologyPanel, or the three showcases; the unused-import cleanup leaves zero lint warnings; all three touched test files pass and assert absence rather than presence. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client lint && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/__tests__/ToolsTab.test.tsx apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/__tests__/AgentRow.test.tsx apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/lib/__tests__/build-topology-elements.test.ts. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 4 (client UI surfaces, file:line verbatim), §5 (dev showcases), §User Experience (Agent settings, Agents list row, Topology view — the honest-absence description of what disappears), §8 (the ToolsTab/AgentRow/build-topology-elements test bullets), Implementation Phases 4. Depends on 1.1 (every typed `.budget`/`manifest.budget` access in these files becomes a compile error once `AgentManifest.budget` is deleted from @dorkos/shared — the schema deletion is what forces this task's hand on the typed sites; the showcase sites are found by the file:line list above since their local types escape the compiler). Runs in parallel with 2.1 (DB migration) and 4.1 (docs/OpenAPI) — no shared files.
tasks.activeForm: Removing every client UI surface that displays or edits the agent budget

tasks.subject: [agent-budget-enforcement] [P4] Delete 3 docs references and regenerate OpenAPI
tasks.description: Delete the three docs references to the agent budget and regenerate OpenAPI so the `AgentBudget` schema and its `$ref`s disappear. docs/concepts/mesh.mdx:125 — delete the budget table row ("Max hops per message… max API calls per hour… Advisory only, not enforced at runtime today."). docs/guides/agents.mdx:63 — delete the `"budget": { … }` line from the example manifest. docs/guides/agent-discovery.mdx:164 — delete the `"budget": { … }` example line; :194-198 — delete the field-table `budget:` entry. Regenerate docs/api/openapi.json via `pnpm docs:export-api` (runs `tsx scripts/export-openapi.ts`, deriving the document from the Zod schemas) — never hand-edit it. Once task 1.1's schema deletion is built (`pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build` first), the `AgentBudget` schema definition (at :4048 in the pre-removal document) and its `$ref`s inside `AgentManifest`/`UpdateAgentRequest` (at :3969, :4187, :4281 in the pre-removal document) disappear automatically on regen — no manual schema edit needed in this task. Confirm the `openapi-fresh` CI check (.github/workflows/docs-openapi-check.yml) is green against this branch; if it comes back red on a change that looks unrelated, merge origin/main first and regenerate again before concluding this task introduced the drift (a red check can also mean another PR already landed a schema-stale main). Acceptance: none of the three MDX files mention budget, `maxHopsPerMessage`, or `maxCallsPerHour`; docs/api/openapi.json is regenerated (not hand-edited) and contains no `AgentBudget` schema or `$ref`; openapi-fresh is green. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build && pnpm docs:export-api && grep -rn "budget\|maxHopsPerMessage\|maxCallsPerHour" docs/concepts/mesh.mdx docs/guides/agents.mdx docs/guides/agent-discovery.mdx (expect no output) && grep -c AgentBudget docs/api/openapi.json (expect 0). Cites: spec §Detailed Design 6 (docs + OpenAPI, file:line verbatim), §Technical Dependencies (pnpm docs:export-api, never hand-edit), §API / OpenAPI deltas. Depends on 1.1 (the regenerated OpenAPI document must reflect the schema already deleted from @dorkos/shared). Runs in parallel with 2.1 (DB migration), 3.1 (client UI), and 5.1 (changelog/ADR) — no shared files.
tasks.activeForm: Deleting the docs references and regenerating OpenAPI

tasks.subject: [agent-budget-enforcement] [P5] Changelog fragment (Changed) + draft ADR — the honesty decision and reintroduction path
tasks.description: Write the two remaining user-facing/decision-record deliverables: the changelog fragment and the draft ADR. CHANGELOG FRAGMENT — create changelog/unreleased/<id>-agent-budget-enforcement.md, where <id> is a timestamp id generated by .claude/scripts/id.ts (filename pattern <YYMMDD-HHMMSS>-agent-budget-enforcement.md per changelog/README.md; no frontmatter; Keep-a-Changelog headings). This is user-visible — an editable control disappears — so it is a small Changed/Removed entry in writing-for-humans voice. Example shape (rewrite in your own plain words, keep it honest): "### Changed\n- Removed the per-agent message and hourly-call limits from agent settings — they were shown as editable controls but never actually limited anything. Runaway protection still comes from the per-message budget, which is enforced. (DOR-265)". Never edit CHANGELOG.md directly (ADR 260707-231641). DRAFT ADR — prefer running /adr:from-spec against specs/agent-budget-enforcement/02-specification.md (it applies the significance rubric at extraction and writes decisions/<id>-<slug>.md plus the decisions/manifest.json entry using a timestamp id from .claude/scripts/id.ts); otherwise write it by hand following the writing-adrs skill. Status: proposed. Title: "Remove the advisory per-agent budget; enforced runaway protection is the per-message envelope budget." Capture: the honesty decision (an editable control that gates nothing violates the AGENTS.md quality bar — "no dark patterns", "no dead code, no tolerated legacy patterns"); the verified single-adapter reality (only the claude-code relay adapter exists today, so multi-adapter enforcement was speculative and out of scope); the backward-tolerance mechanism (Zod's default unknown-key strip in readManifest plus the file-first write-through — a stale on-disk budget key is silently dropped on read and removed from disk on the next write, no migration needed); and the correct in-adapter turn-start signal for any future enforcement — inside the claude-code adapter, after the STREAM_EVENT_TYPES skip (packages/relay/src/adapters/claude-code/agent-handler.ts:179-204), right before agentManager.sendMessage, NOT naively counting relay.agent.* publishes at the relay gate (which would burn the cap on reply/stream traffic). Cross-link: DOR-260 (made the per-message envelope budget RelayBudget authoritative and enforced — the reason this per-agent budget is redundant dead metadata); ADR-0043 (file-first agent storage — the reason dropping budget_json is safe and the reason strip-on-read plus write-through cleanly retires the on-disk key). Add the decisions/manifest.json entry with status proposed and a timestamp id from .claude/scripts/id.ts. Acceptance: the changelog fragment exists with a valid timestamp-id filename and a Changed or Removed heading, and reads plainly and honestly (no hype, no fabricated claim); decisions/<id>-<slug>.md exists with status proposed, capturing context/decision/consequences and the DOR-260 + ADR-0043 cross-links; decisions/manifest.json is valid JSON with the new proposed entry. Verify: node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('decisions/manifest.json','utf8'))" (confirms it parses) plus a manual writing-for-humans self-check (no automated prose test) plus confirming the changelog filename matches changelog/README.md's <id>-<slug>.md pattern. Cites: spec §Documentation (the changelog fragment's exact draft copy), §Related ADRs (the Proposed ADR's full content list), §Reintroduction path (the in-adapter turn-start signal the ADR must pin so the lesson is not relearned). Depends on 3.1 (the changelog describes the shipped UI removal — "an editable control disappears" — so it is written once that surface is actually gone, matching the repo's own precedent of sequencing user-facing prose after the surfaces it describes). Runs in parallel with 2.1, 4.1, and 6.1 — prose work shares no files with the DB migration, docs/OpenAPI regen, or the fixture sweep.
tasks.activeForm: Writing the changelog fragment and drafting the ADR

tasks.subject: [agent-budget-enforcement] [P6] Test-fixture sweep (~28 files) + legacy-tolerance round-trip test + clean rebuild + full verify gate
tasks.description: Sweep the remaining ~28 test fixtures that merely include a budget field (no assertion on it), add the new legacy-tolerance round-trip test, clear the stale build residue, and run the full-suite green gate — the final proof that the removal is complete everywhere. FIXTURE SWEEP — task 1.1 already fixed the three mesh tests that assert on budget (agent-registry.test.ts, relay-integration.test.ts, reconciler.test.ts) and their shared mock-manifest factory (mock-factories.ts); task 2.1 already rewrote migrations.test.ts; task 3.1 already fixed ToolsTab.test.tsx, AgentRow.test.tsx, and build-topology-elements.test.ts. This task covers the remaining ~28 files that merely carry a budget: field in an AgentManifest/AgentRegistryEntry-shaped fixture and need the field dropped, no assertion to rewrite: server (a2a-routes, agents, agents-conventions, context-builder, context-builder-conventions, mcp-resources, mcp-structured-output, ensure-dorkbot — under apps/server/src), client (agent-hooks, BindingDialog, tab-migration-parity, IdentityTab, PersonalityTab, ChannelsTab, AgentsList, agent-filter-schema, OfflineAgentDetailSheet, ConversationRow, TaskRow, AgentsPage — under apps/client/src), and packages (a2a-gateway ×3, mesh manifest, mesh-core, reconciler [fixture-only files beyond the assertion already fixed in 1.1], relay-bridge, topology, discovery/unified-scanner, shared manifest — under packages/*/src). Run pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build && pnpm verify FIRST to get the compiler's authoritative red-file list — the sweep is compiler-guided, not grep-guided (every one of these fixtures is typed AgentManifest, so the deleted budget field turns each into a compile error); use that list as the definitive scope, cross-checked against the ~28 names above. LEGACY-TOLERANCE TEST (new) — packages/shared/src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts: write an agent.json containing a top-level "budget": { "maxHopsPerMessage": 5, "maxCallsPerHour": 100 } plus valid required fields; assert readManifest (manifest.ts:59) succeeds (non-null), the returned manifest has NO budget property, and no warning was logged (distinguishing the strip path from the invalid-manifest path). Then writeManifest (manifest.ts:85) the returned manifest and re-read the file bytes to assert budget is absent on disk — proving strip-on-read plus removed-on-next-write, the zero-touch backward-compat contract: old agent.json files carrying a stale budget key load fine, forever, with no migration of on-disk files required. STALE BUILD RESIDUE — clear packages/mesh/dist/budget-mapper.{d.ts,js,js.map,d.ts.map} and dist/__tests__/budget-mapper.test.* (git-ignored, uncommitted; no source file backs them — the former BudgetMapper enforcer was already deleted as dead code) via a clean rebuild: rm -rf packages/mesh/dist && pnpm --filter @dorkos/mesh build. No committed change results; this only prevents the stale .d.ts from lingering in a working tree and confusing knip or a future reader. FULL-SUITE GATE — pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build first (a stale dist causes false-red type errors), then pnpm verify (affected typecheck/lint/test), then pnpm test -- --run for the full green suite (never bare pnpm vitest run for a full run — it falsely fails two tests in the dev environment). Confirm zero remaining references anywhere in packages/ and apps/ via grep -rn "AgentBudget\|maxHopsPerMessage\|maxCallsPerHour" packages apps --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" (expect no output outside the intentionally-descriptive changelog/decisions prose, which lives outside packages/apps anyway). Acceptance: every fixture in the ~28-file list compiles with no budget field; the new legacy-tolerance test passes and proves strip-on-read plus removed-on-next-write; packages/mesh/dist carries no budget-mapper residue after the clean rebuild; pnpm test -- --run is green end to end; the repo-wide grep returns nothing. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build && pnpm verify && pnpm vitest run packages/shared/src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts && rm -rf packages/mesh/dist && pnpm --filter @dorkos/mesh build && pnpm test -- --run. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 8 (the full ~36-file test-fixture sweep, compiler-guided — this task's ~28-file remainder after 1.1/2.1/3.1's ~8), §Detailed Design 7 (stale build residue), §Backward compatibility (the pinned strip-on-read/removed-on-write contract), §Testing Strategy (the legacy-tolerance test's exact assertions; the full-suite gate), Implementation Phases 6. Depends on 1.1, 2.1, 3.1, and 4.1 — this is the final everything-green gate, not an independent slice: it needs the schema deletion, the DB migration, the client UI removal, and the docs/OpenAPI regen all landed so the compiler-guided sweep and the full-suite verify run against the finished tree. Runs in parallel with 5.1 — the changelog/ADR prose shares no files with the fixture sweep.
tasks.activeForm: Sweeping the remaining test fixtures and running the full verify gate
